seoTitle: How to Add Categories in Jira for Organization
metaDescription: Learn how to create and assign project categories in Jira to organize projects into logical groups and improve navigation.
intro: As the number of projects in a Jira instance grows, keeping them organized becomes essential for navigation and reporting. This guide covers creating project categories in Jira, assigning projects to them, and managing categories over time.

steps.heading: Open project settings
steps.body: Navigate to the administration area where project categories are managed.

steps.heading: Create a project category
steps.body: Add a new category with a name that reflects how you want to group your projects, such as by department or product line.

steps.heading: Assign a category to a project
steps.body: Open a project's settings and select the category you just created to associate it with that project.

steps.heading: Manage and edit categories
steps.body: Return to the categories admin area anytime to rename, reassign, or remove categories as your organization changes.

faq.question: How do I add a category in Jira?
faq.answer: Go to project settings, create a new project category, and then assign it to the relevant projects.

faq.question: Who can create project categories in Jira?
faq.answer: Creating and managing project categories typically requires Jira administrator permissions.

faq.question: Can a project belong to more than one category?
faq.answer: No, a project is generally assigned to a single category at a time to keep organization clear.

faq.question: Why would I organize projects into categories?
faq.answer: Categories make it easier to navigate and report on projects, especially in larger Jira instances with many active projects.
